req.title: Schedule performance review — variant 3
req.statement: A manager shall schedule a performance review with the reviewee at least 7 days in advance; past or sub-7-day dates shall be rejected. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines a scheduling constraint (>= 7 days in advance), a 422 error path for violations, and a webhook emission obligation. I derived five tests: (1) EP-positive — a date well inside the valid partition (now+14d) confirms happy-path scheduling, 201 response, and webhook fired; (2) NEG-negative — a date in the past confirms 422 rejection; (3) BVA-boundary exact-7d — scheduled_at == now+7d is the inclusive lower bound, must succeed; (4) BVA-boundary sub-7d — scheduled_at == now+6d+23h59m is the exclusive boundary, must be rejected with 422; (5) STC — verifies the state machine moves from 'draft' through 'scheduled' to 'notified' after a valid submission. Each test is self-contained via fixtures and uses deterministic UTC timestamps anchored to test-run time.

tests.name: test_schedule_review_valid_date_succeeds
preconditions: Authenticated manager session token available via <manager_auth_fixture>
A valid reviewee employee ID available via <employee_fixture>
actions: POST /performance-reviews/schedule with manager token, reviewee_id, and scheduled_at
Assert HTTP 201 and response body contains review id
Assert webhook sink received exactly one 'review.scheduled' event
expected: Webhook event 'review.scheduled' received with matching review id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_valid_date_succeeds(manager_auth_fixture, employee_fixture, webhook_sink_fixture, api_client):
    scheduled_at = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=14)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "reviewee_id": employee_fixture["id"],
        "scheduled_at": scheduled_at,
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/performance-reviews/schedule",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_auth_fixture['token']}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "scheduled"
    events = webhook_sink_fixture.get_events(event_type="review.scheduled")
    assert len(events) == 1
    assert events[0]["data"]["review_id"] == body["id"]

tests.name: test_schedule_review_past_date_rejected
preconditions: Authenticated manager session token available via <manager_auth_fixture>
A valid reviewee employee ID available via <employee_fixture>
actions: Compute scheduled_at = UTC now - 1 day (past date)
POST /performance-reviews/schedule with manager token, reviewee_id, and scheduled_at
Assert HTTP 422 and error body references scheduling constraint
expected: Response JSON contains error field referencing 'scheduled_at' constraint
trace: R-WEB-094: error pathway — past date must return 422
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_past_date_rejected(manager_auth_fixture, employee_fixture, webhook_sink_fixture, api_client):
    scheduled_at = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "reviewee_id": employee_fixture["id"],
        "scheduled_at": scheduled_at,
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/performance-reviews/schedule",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_auth_fixture['token']}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "scheduled_at" in str(body)
    events = webhook_sink_fixture.get_events(event_type="review.scheduled")
    assert len(events) == 0

preconditions: Authenticated manager session token available via <manager_auth_fixture>
A valid reviewee employee ID available via <employee_fixture>
actions: Compute scheduled_at = UTC now + exactly 7 days (to the second)
POST /performance-reviews/schedule with manager token, reviewee_id, and scheduled_at
Assert HTTP 201 — inclusive lower bound must be accepted
trace: R-WEB-094: boundary value — scheduled_at == now+7d is valid inclusive bound

        "scheduled_at": scheduled_at,

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/performance-reviews/schedule",

        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_auth_fixture['token']}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "scheduled"
    events = webhook_sink_fixture.get_events(event_type="review.scheduled")
    assert len(events) == 1

tests.name: test_schedule_review_sub_7_days_boundary_rejected
preconditions: Authenticated manager session token available via <manager_auth_fixture>
A valid reviewee employee ID available via <employee_fixture>
actions: Compute scheduled_at = UTC now + 7 days - 1 second (just below inclusive bound)
POST /performance-reviews/schedule with manager token, reviewee_id, and scheduled_at
Assert HTTP 422 — exclusive lower boundary must be rejected
expected: Response error references 'scheduled_at' minimum advance constraint
trace: R-WEB-094: boundary value — scheduled_at == now+7d-1s is invalid (sub-7d)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_sub_7_days_boundary_rejected(manager_auth_fixture, employee_fixture, webhook_sink_fixture, api_client):
    scheduled_at = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=7) - timedelta(seconds=1)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "reviewee_id": employee_fixture["id"],
        "scheduled_at": scheduled_at,
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/performance-reviews/schedule",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_auth_fixture['token']}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "scheduled_at" in str(body)
    events = webhook_sink_fixture.get_events(event_type="review.scheduled")
    assert len(events) == 0

tests.name: test_schedule_review_state_transitions_draft_to_notified
preconditions: Authenticated manager session token available via <manager_auth_fixture>
A valid reviewee employee ID available via <employee_fixture>
actions: Verify no existing review in 'scheduled' state for reviewee via GET /performance-reviews
Assert response status transitions: draft -> scheduled
Assert notification mock received participant notification
Assert webhook event 'review.scheduled' emitted confirming full state path
expected: HTTP 201 and initial status 'scheduled' (draft->scheduled transition confirmed)
Notification service received notification for both manager and reviewee
Webhook event 'review.scheduled' present with correct actor and reviewee ids
trace: R-WEB-094: state transition — review moves draft->scheduled->notified
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_schedule_review_state_transitions_draft_to_notified(manager_auth_fixture, employee_fixture, webhook_sink_fixture, notification_mock_fixture, api_client):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_auth_fixture['token']}"}
    scheduled_at = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=10)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "reviewee_id": employee_fixture["id"],
        "scheduled_at": scheduled_at,
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/performance-reviews/schedule", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    review_id = body["id"]
    assert body["status"] == "scheduled"
    notifications = notification_mock_fixture.get_notifications(review_id=review_id)
    notified_ids = {n["recipient_id"] for n in notifications}
    assert manager_auth_fixture["user_id"] in notified_ids
    assert employee_fixture["id"] in notified_ids
    events = webhook_sink_fixture.get_events(event_type="review.scheduled")
    assert any(e["data"]["review_id"] == review_id for e in events)
